results.rendered_html:
nāgarika (von nagara)
1〉 adj.
a〉 städtisch, in der Stadt wohnend; subst. Städter: nāgarikāraṇyajanayoḥ saṃbandho nopapadyate Schol. zu  ŚĀK. 51 .  DAŚAK. _in_ BENF. Chr. 180,14 . 188,15 . ˚bala 187,4 .  R. 2,83,10 .  VIKR. 77,12 .  MĀRK. P. 8,50 .
— b〉 von einer Stadt einkommend; n. viell. Stadtabgabe  P. 4,3,75 , Sch.
— c〉 höflich, artig: nāgarikavṛttyā saṃjñāpayainām  ŚĀK. 60,2 .
— d〉 gewandt, klug, gerieben  PAÑCAT. ed. orn. I, 26 .
— 2〉 m. Stadthaupt, Polizeimeister  ŚĀK. 73,1 .
— Vgl. nāgaraka.

results.rendered_html:
nāgarika 1〉 adj. f. ā (könnte auch zu nāgaraka gezogen werden)
a〉  KATHĀS. 124,162. fg.   SĀH. D. 173,5 . nāgarikāḥ (= gūrjaryaḥ) striyaḥ  Verz. d. Oxf. H. 217,b,16 .
— c〉 nāgarikokti  KATHĀS. 71,28 . ˚vṛtta  Verz. d. Oxf. H. 215,b,23 . m. ein höflicher —, feingebildeter Städter 216,a,31 .  Spr. 2990 .
— d〉 zu streichen, da die Stelle zu c〉 gehört; vgl.  Spr. 4227 .
— 2〉  DAŚAK. _in_ BENF. Chr. 195,13 .

results.rendered_html:
nāgaraka, nāgarika a. [nagare bhavaḥ vuñ]
1 Town-bred, town-born.
2 Polite, courteous, courtly; nāgarikavṛttyā saṃjñāpayaināṃ  Ś. 5 ; sādhu ārya nāgarikosi  V. 2. 
3 Clever, shrewd, cunning (vidagdha).
— kaḥ
1 A citizen.
2 A polite or courteous man, a gallant; one who shows exaggerated attention to his first mistress while he is courting some one else.
3 One who has contracted the vices of a town.
4 A thief.
5 An artist.
6 The chief of the police;  V. 5 ;  Ś. 6. 
— kaṃ Dry ginger.
— (rikaṃ) The toll levied from a town.
